hero.title:
Welcome to
Speedcubing Belgium
hero.subtitle: Join the fastest growing puzzle sport in Belgium

about.title:
What is
Speedcubing Belgium?
paragraphs: Speedcubing Belgium (SCB) is the official representative organization for speedcubing in Belgium, working in close collaboration with the World Cube Association (WCA).
We organize and oversee official speedcubing competitions throughout Belgium, where cubers of all skill levels compete to solve various twisty puzzles as fast as possible.
Whether you're a beginner who just learned to solve a Rubik's Cube or an experienced speedcuber chasing national records, SCB provides a welcoming community and competitive platform for all.

items.title: Organize Competitions
items.description: We organize official WCA competitions across Belgium, ensuring fair play and professional event management.

items.title: Promote Speedcubing
items.description: We work to raise awareness about speedcubing through demonstrations, workshops, and community outreach.

items.title: Build Community
items.description: We foster a welcoming community where cubers can meet, share techniques, and support each other's growth.

joinCta.title: Ready to Join Us?
joinCta.subtitle: Check out our upcoming competitions and become part of the Belgian speedcubing scene!

hero.title: About Us
hero.subtitle: Learn more about Speedcubing Belgium and our mission to promote competitive cubing across the country.

events.title: The Beginning
events.description: Speedcubing Belgium was founded by passionate cubers who wanted to create an organized structure for competitive cubing in Belgium.

events.title: First Major Competition
events.description: We hosted our first large-scale WCA competition, bringing together cubers from across the country.

events.title: Community Growth
events.description: Our community expanded significantly, with new delegates joining and more competitions being organized nationwide.

events.title: Record Breaking Year
events.description: Belgian cubers achieved multiple national records, putting Belgium on the map in the international speedcubing scene.

events.title: Expanding Horizons
events.description: We introduced new event formats and expanded our reach to underrepresented regions in Belgium.

events.title: Today
events.description: We proudly serve hundreds of active competitors and continue to grow the Belgian speedcubing community.

mission.title: Promoting Speedcubing in Belgium
mission.description: To promote and develop speedcubing in Belgium by organizing high-quality competitions, fostering a vibrant community, and supporting cubers of all levels in their pursuit of excellence.

getInvolved.title: Get
getInvolved.subtitle: Want to contribute to the Belgian speedcubing community? There are many ways to get involved!

hero.title: Upcoming Competitions
hero.subtitle: Join us at official WCA-sanctioned speedcubing competitions across Belgium. All skill levels welcome!

upcoming.title: Upcoming Competitions
upcoming.empty: No upcoming competitions at the moment. Check back soon!

firstTime.title: First Time
firstTime.subtitle: Don't worry! Our competitions are beginner-friendly. Here's what you need to know:
tips: You must be able to solve a Rubik's Cube (any speed is fine!)
Registration is done through the World Cube Association website
Bring your own cubes — we recommend having at least one backup
Competitors help judge and scramble for each other — it's part of the experience!

hero.title: National Records
hero.subtitle: Celebrating the fastest times achieved by Belgian speedcubers in official WCA competitions.

info.title: About National
info.description: National records are officially recognized by the World Cube Association (WCA) and must be set at official WCA competitions held in Belgium.
facts: Single: The fastest single solve in an event
Average: The average of 5 solves (removing best and worst)
All times are verified by official WCA delegates and judges

about.heading: About SCB
about.description: Speedcubing Belgium is the official representative organization for competitive Rubik's Cube solving in Belgium, affiliated with the World Cube Association (WCA).
